title: @fastify/cors registered with origin: "*" — any website can call the API with the user's session

params.passDetail: @fastify/cors is registered with an explicit allow-list origin (not "*") — cross-origin access is restricted to trusted sites.
params.failDetail: @fastify/cors is registered with origin: "*", allowing ANY website to make cross-origin requests to this API. Combined with credentials this lets a malicious page act as the logged-in user. Set origin to an explicit allow-list (a string, array, or function), not "*".
params.absentCallDetail: No plugin register call in this scope; this rule does not apply here.
params.absentArgDetail: The @fastify/cors registration does not pass an origin literal; verify the resolved origin is a trusted allow-list, not "*".

provenance.note: The Fastify equivalent of the Express cors wildcard footgun. origin: "*" is pasted to make CORS errors go away in development and ships to production, opening the API to any origin.
